test.name: Extract assertions from the 1910 census — John Becker household (stated relationships)
test.description: 1910 U.S. Census household of John Becker, Cincinnati Ward 7, Hamilton County, Ohio (ark:/61903/1:1:MLFM-GC8). Real record from the Ferber/Becker research (#799). Post-1880: has a relationship-to-head column, so relationships are DIRECT; but records age (not birth date), so birth years are indirect.

input.user_message:
Extract assertions from this 1910 U.S. Census household:

United States, Census, 1910 - Household of John Becker
Cincinnati Ward 7, Hamilton, Ohio, United States
ark:/61903/1:1:MLFM-GC8

Relationship - Name - Sex - Birth year - Birthplace - Marital status:
- Head: John Becker, male, born about 1845, Kentucky, Widowed
- Daughter: Louise Becker, female, born about 1873, Ohio, Single
- Son: Edward Becker, male, born about 1877, Ohio, Single
- Son: Frank Becker, male, born about 1889, Ohio, Single

(1910 U.S. Census - includes a relationship-to-head-of-household column; records age, not a birth date.)

judge_context: The 1910 census HAS a relationship-to-head column, so John as father of Louise, Edward, and Frank is DIRECT evidence - stated, not inferred
The 1910 census records AGE, not a birth date, so birth years are derived from age and are therefore INDIRECT (unlike the 1900 census, which stated month/year and is direct)
Extract John's birthplace exactly as stated ('Kentucky'); the conflict with the 1870 household's 'Ohio' is downstream, not an extraction fix
Only extract the people enumerated here - do NOT add Clara (present in the 1900 household) or anyone not listed on this record
Atomic assertions per person; pre-1940 census informant is unknown so information quality is indeterminate; the enumerator is the recorder
